context: Tests whether the agent applies minimal, targeted fixes to the plugin — adding explicit retry timing rather than vague guidance, matching the rubric's exact language, and leaving every unrelated working section untouched without restructuring or bloating.

checklist.name: Explicit retry intervals
checklist.description: The updated SKILL.md specifies concrete delay values for retries (e.g. '1s, 2s, 4s' or specific millisecond values) rather than vague phrases like 'increasing delays' or 'wait between retries'

checklist.name: Rubric language used
checklist.description: The added retry content uses 'exponential backoff' (the exact term from eval_criteria.json) rather than paraphrases like 'progressive delay' or 'back-off strategy'

checklist.name: HMAC section unchanged
checklist.description: The HMAC-SHA256 signature validation instruction in the Setup section is preserved verbatim or functionally identical

checklist.name: TLS section unchanged
checklist.description: The TLS 1.2 configuration line in the Setup section is not modified or removed

checklist.name: Observability section unchanged
checklist.description: The Observability section (structured log entry with event type, source IP, duration, outcome) is not modified

checklist.name: Processing section unchanged
checklist.description: The four numbered steps in the Processing section are still present in the same order in the updated plugin

checklist.name: Retry section only changed
checklist.description: Only the Reliability section (or equivalent retry-specific section) was substantively modified; Setup and Processing sections are structurally intact

checklist.name: Concise addition
checklist.description: The Reliability section (or equivalent) does not exceed 100 words in the updated plugin

checklist.name: Max retry count preserved
checklist.description: The 3-attempt maximum retry limit is still present in the updated plugin (not removed or changed to a different number)

checklist.name: Fast acknowledgement preserved
checklist.description: The instruction to acknowledge receipt with a 200 response quickly (before heavy processing) is still present
